chapter_info.subject: Science
chapter_info.chapter_name: Mindful Eating: A Path to a Healthy Body

flashcards.q: Term: Wholesome, moderate food appropriate for time/place.

flashcards.a: Food gives life to living beings.

flashcards.q: Carbohydrate: Type detected by iodine test.
Fat: Purpose of fat accumulation in polar bears.
Protein: Main function in the body.
flashcards.a: Growth and repair of body (muscle building).

flashcards.q: Mineral: Deficiency causing swelling of the neck (Goitre).
Mineral: Lack of this in blood leads to Anemia/fatigue.
Balanced Diet: Three essential non-nutrient components.
Roughage: Main function in the body.
flashcards.a: Helping get rid of undigested food (smooth stools).

flashcards.q: Food Test: Positive result for starch with iodine.
Food Test: Positive result for protein chemicals.
Food Test: Positive result for fat rub.

flashcards.a: High calorie foods low in protein/minerals/vitamins.
Distance food travels from producer to consumer.

flashcards.q: Food Waste: Major governmental school meal scheme.
Nutrient Loss: Why wash fruits before cutting, not after?
flashcards.a: To avoid losing water-soluble vitamins like Vitamin C.
Adding extra nutrients to food during processing.
Regulates food safety and quality standards.

flashcards.q: Water: Two main ways waste is removed from the body.
